summary.intent: Investigate and fix how Moltis reads skills, addressing a bug where the agent attempts to use MCP filesystem tools to read skill files instead of loading them directly, causing it to hallucinate or fail.
summary.outcome: The codebase was explored to understand the skills loading mechanism, but no fix was implemented — only the chat-input E2E spec was modified, suggesting the session ended before a solution was applied.

repo: Moltis is a Rust workspace with separate crates: skills, tools, web, etc.
Skills are stored in a .moltis/skills folder and represented by SKILL.md files
The skills crate has lib.rs and prompt_gen.rs as key files for skill loading logic
skill_tools.rs in the tools crate handles skill-related tool definitions exposed to the agent
The agent was incorrectly routing skill reads through MCP filesystem tools rather than a native/direct loading path

code.finding: Core skill loading library; entry point for how skills are discovered and loaded
Handles prompt generation from skills, likely where SKILL.md content is embedded into prompts
Defines tool interfaces for skill access; suspected root cause of the MCP filesystem tool being invoked instead of direct skill reads

workflow: Screenshots from users are a useful diagnostic signal for reproducing agent fugue states
Grepping for read_skill/load_skill/activate patterns is the right approach to trace skill loading flow in this codebase

friction: Agent enters a 'fugue state' when it cannot load a skill — it hallucinates content from memory or other readable files rather than failing cleanly
MCP filesystem tool is being invoked for skill reads, which is unintended and causes the failure path
Root cause was not fully pinned down in this session — skill_tools.rs was read but no fix was landed
open_items: Fix skill loading to bypass MCP filesystem tools and read SKILL.md files directly via the skills crate
Add error handling so the agent fails gracefully (not hallucinates) when a skill file cannot be found
Understand why crates/web/ui/e2e/specs/chat-input.spec.js was modified — may be unrelated or a side effect
Verify the fix works end-to-end using the user's reproduction case shown in the screenshots
